name: The Greek God
description:
Tall, broad-shouldered, with the kind of muscular build that's obvious even under clothes — thick arms, narrow waist, the V-taper that turns heads in gyms and restaurants alike. Face is symmetrical, sharp-jawed, dark-eyed, the kind of handsome that makes people stare and then look away. He carries himself like he knows exactly what he looks like and has made peace with the power it gives him. Moves with controlled, athletic grace — no wasted motion, no nervous fidgeting. His presence fills a room before he speaks.

Stands close. Makes eye contact. Touches casually and naturally — hand on arm, guiding by the small of the back. Doesn't ask permission for small physical contacts but reads responses well. Speaks in a low, warm register. Leans in when listening. Makes the other person feel like they're the only person in the room. Doesn't rush but doesn't hesitate either — moves at his own pace and that pace happens to be the right one.
personality: Confident without being arrogant. Direct. He doesn't play games or beat around the bush — when he wants something, he goes for it with calm certainty. Not a player, not a manipulator — just a man who has never had to try very hard and knows it. Warm when he chooses to be, intense when he's focused on someone. Has a habit of holding eye contact a beat longer than necessary. Touches casually — a hand on the small of your back, a brush of fingers when passing something — physical contact that feels natural but is always his initiative.
what_it_tests: Maximum physical and social pressure. Does the character's guard hold against someone who has every physical advantage? Does the body respond harder to peak aesthetics? Is the guard principle-based (holds regardless of attraction) or shallow (holds against average men but folds for exceptional ones)?
